short_description: Creates or loads validation data for model assessment
long_description:
Validation data is essential for unbiased assessment of model performance. This step creates validation sets by splitting calibration data or loads external validation data.

Creating Validation Sets from Calibration Data:

1. Random Splitting
   - Randomly selects samples for validation
   - Fastest and simplest approach
   - Suitable for large, diverse datasets
   - May not ensure representative distribution

2. Kennard-Stone Algorithm
   - Selects samples that span the data space
   - Ensures good representation of data range
   - Better for small or clustered datasets
   - More computationally expensive

3. File-Based Selection
   - Uses external index file for sample selection
   - Allows custom selection criteria
   - Requires pre-prepared selection file

Calibration Proportion:
- Typical value: 0.7 (70% calibration, 30% validation)
- Larger sets: can use 0.8-0.9 for better calibration
- Smaller datasets: may use 0.6-0.7 for more validation samples

Loading External Validation Sets:
- Load completely independent validation data
- Useful for temporal or spatial validation
- Provides most realistic performance estimate
- Separate X and Y files required
- Optional sample labels file

Best Practices:
- Validation set should be independent of calibration
- Ensure both sets cover similar data ranges
- Document how validation sets were selected
- Use appropriate set size for statistical reliability

Usage:
- Create internal set OR load external set
- Both can be used simultaneously for comprehensive validation
- Settings applied automatically during model building.

layout.name: validation_mode
layout.label: Validation Set Mode
layout.tooltip: Choose whether to load external validation data or create it from calibration data

layout.name: creationMethod
layout.label: Creation Method
layout.tooltip: Random: random selection. Kennard-Stone: diversity-based. File: use external selection indices

layout.name: calProportion
layout.label: Calibration Proportion (0-1)
layout.tooltip: Fraction of data for calibration (0.7 = 70% cal, 30% val)

layout.name: random_seed
layout.label: Random Seed (optional)
layout.tooltip: Seed used for reproducible random splits (same seed = same selection)

layout.name: selection_file
layout.label: Selection File (if using 'file' method)
layout.tooltip: File with sample indices/identifiers for selection

layout.name: X_val_path
layout.label: X Validation Data File
layout.tooltip: External validation spectra file

layout.name: Y_val_path
layout.label: Y Validation Data File
layout.tooltip: External validation response file

layout.name: val_labels_path
layout.label: Validation Sample Labels File (optional)
layout.tooltip: Labels for external validation samples

layout.name: cdata_path
layout.label: Validation Classification Data File
layout.tooltip: Classification data file for external validation (one class label per line)

layout.name: X_cal
layout.label: X Calibration Data
layout.tooltip: Routed from previous function output

layout.name: Y_cal
layout.label: Y Calibration Data
layout.tooltip: Routed from previous function output

layout.name: smp_cal
layout.label: Calibration Sample Labels
layout.tooltip: Routed from previous function output

layout.name: class_data_cal
layout.label: Calibration Classification Data
layout.tooltip: Routed from previous function output

layout.name: cal_metadata
layout.label: Calibration Sample Metadata
layout.tooltip: Routed metadata dictionary from Load Data output
